behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure boundary is handler + signature verification + queue handoff (must prove 'not forwarded'). Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit is too low since this behavior is not only digest comparison; it also asserts no cross-module enqueue call. Upgrade check: System rejected because behavior text only requires HTTP status and no-forwarding logic, not real network/OS/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler in-process with real signature-check code and a spy/fake queue module. Send a request with mismatched `X-Signature-256`; assert `401` and assert queue `enqueue` was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is ordering across local modules (HTTP handler and queue persistence call). Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit cannot verify ordering at module handoff. Upgrade check: System rejected; behavior text does not require real network/OS effects, only relative ordering of enqueue completion before response.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process HTTP handler and a controllable queue adapter whose write promise resolves on command. Assert response is not sent before write resolves; then resolve write and assert `200` is returned.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is async boundary wiring between handler enqueue path and downstream processor completion. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit would miss real handoff timing between modules. Upgrade check: System rejected; behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess, only non-blocking sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream processor stub in-process. Make processor block (unresolved promise), send valid event, assert HTTP success returns while processor is still pending.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: System, because behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrent request load, which is sensitive to runtime scheduling and real HTTP serving behavior. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration could validate logic but can miss end-to-end timing failures under actual server concurrency. Upgrade check: Agentic rejected since no AI/ML output dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real HTTP server, seed concurrent earlier events whose downstream processing is intentionally slow, then send a new valid webhook and measure real elapsed time at the client; assert response is received in under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because dedupe correctness is at boundary between handler/idempotency check and queue write path. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit is too low since behavior requires proving no second write across modules. Upgrade check: System rejected; no real network/OS/subprocess requirement appears in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same event ID twice through in-process handler wiring with real dedupe logic and queue adapter. Assert first request writes once, second request does not produce another queue write.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is error mapping across queue module and HTTP response layer. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit might test status mapping function but misses real module handoff from queue-full condition. Upgrade check: System rejected; behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess, only propagation of a queue-full signal to HTTP output.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with queue module configured to return a 'full' error/result. Send valid webhook and assert HTTP `503` plus `Retry-After` header is present.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this validates the real queue implementation against configured capacity using local persistence wiring. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit with mocked storage can hide true enforcement failures. Upgrade check: System considered but rejected since behavior text does not require external network/subprocess; local persistent store exercise fits Integration scope.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue component with a small capacity in test storage, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more and assert explicit rejection/full error.
